target.name: Gallatin AI
target.founder: Woody Glier, Daniel Buchmueller, Brian Ballard

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Gallatin AI is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Woody Glier, Daniel Buchmueller, and Brian Ballard that has raised $15M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 10 private companies (including Anduril Industries, Helsing, and Shield AI) and 2 public peers in the Defense & Security sector, though a narrow peer set at identical stage limits direct comparable company analysis.

Stage-progression methodology is the applicable triangulation approach here. Using total capital raised ($15M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-Series A step-ups yields an implied valuation of $38M. This method is grounded in observed progression patterns within the Defense & Security cohort, though applied cautiously given the nascent stage and limited operational history. A peer-median approach was not feasible; while 19 same-sector peers exist, fewer than three match Gallatin's stage and tier simultaneously, making a narrow-set median unreliable.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $32M to $43M, with a central estimate of $38M. This range reflects ±15% variance around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the maturity and defensibility of the underlying technology, customer traction in Defense & Security procurement cycles, and whether the $15M raised represents a pre- or post-money construct. Given reliance on a single method, triangulation remains incomplete pending additional financial or operational data.
